steps.title: Evaluate the function at x = 0
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs where $x = 0$. For $f(x) = -8(5)^x + \frac{1}{k}$, substitute $x = 0$:
$$f(0) = -8(5)^0 + \frac{1}{k}$$
Since $5^0 = 1$:
$$f(0) = -8(1) + \frac{1}{k} = -8 + \frac{1}{k}$$

steps.title: Combine into a single fraction
steps.content:
Express both terms with denominator $k$:
$$-8 + \frac{1}{k} = \frac{-8k}{k} + \frac{1}{k} = \frac{-8k + 1}{k} = \frac{1 - 8k}{k}$$
That confirms B is correct.
